text.title: Pricing Experiment Procedure
text.summary: 7-step procedure to design, launch, run, and close a Stripe-based pricing experiment.

step.name: baseline-snapshot
step.input: 90 days of MRR, trial-to-paid conversion, churn rate, refund_rate, dispute_rate.
step.action: Export from Stripe + analytics. Persist baseline values to plan.baseline. Required for guardrail thresholds.

step.name: pre-commit-plan
step.action: Fill templates/experiment-plan.md. Specify experiment_id, dates, duration (7-90d), variant_old / variant_new, audience, exposure_rule, success_metric, significance_target, guardrails, grandfathering policy, currencies, tax_behavior, termination rules.
step.output: experiment-plan.md committed to the marketing repo BEFORE any Stripe change.
step.decision-gate: Plan missing any required field → block. r1.

step.name: grandfathering-pin
step.action: Create either (a) a separate Stripe Price object that existing subs stay on indefinitely, or (b) a 100% off coupon stacked on the new Price for the grandfather window. Attach coupon to all eligible customer IDs. Record stripe_coupon_id in Plan.
step.decision-gate: Old Stripe Price object must remain active — never archive or delete. r2.

step.name: stripe-config-build
step.action: Create new Price object per currency in plan.currencies_in_test. Set tax_behavior. Wire pricing-page A/B router (random hash, geo split, or cohort flag).

step.name: pre-flight-validation
step.action: Run scripts/validate-pricing-experiment-runbook.py against the artefact bundle. Run scripts/stripe-pre-flight.py (or equivalent) against production Stripe to verify: new Price exists, old Price active, coupon attached to eligible customers, no orphan subs, no test-mode artefacts in live.
step.decision-gate: Pre-flight not green within 7 days of plan.start_date → block launch. r3.

step.name: customer-announcement-and-run
step.action: Send customer-facing announcement explaining the change and grandfather rules. Flip the pricing-page router. Monitor guardrails daily. Trigger termination rule if refund_rate > 2× baseline OR dispute_rate > 0.5%.
step.output: Experiment running with daily guardrail readouts.

step.name: forced-decision-memo
step.action: Within 48h of end_date, write templates/decision-memo.md: metric readouts per variant, significance verdict, qualitative signals, single binary decision (adopt_new / revert_to_old / extend_with_adjustment with named change). Commit. Post a one-paragraph customer announcement of the outcome.
step.output: decision-memo.md committed; pricing-page router updated to the chosen variant.
step.decision-gate: Third extend_with_adjustment in a row → reject. Force adopt or revert. r5.
